page: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The configuration features 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core) and 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB. It includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. The Nitro hypervisor manages the instance, which lacks GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several areas. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and a multi-core score of 21428.0, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Software compilation with Clang scored 59025.0, and static web serving throughput reached 109.49 GB/sec, both falling into the top-tier category. In stress-ng testing, multi-core CPU performance was strong at 69215.04, while single-core performance was average at 2120.1. Memory bandwidth testing showed strong read-only performance at 5134.19 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 196.82 GB/sec for large blocks, though large block write bandwidth was average at 83.78 GB/sec.

The hardware profile and benchmark results make this server suitable for compute-heavy workloads, software compilation, and static web serving. The inclusion of 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage supports database operations, where it achieved a strong Passmark database score of 29890.99 and 112.86 million Redis SET operations per second. For machine learning, the CPU supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), enabling top-tier prompt processing speeds of 39.27 tokens per second on a large 70B LLM model, though text generation on the same model is limited to an average speed of 3.48 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server utilizing a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) and 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating at 3.9 GHz, the CPU features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX. The instance is equipped with 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 30 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in software compilation, image processing, and static web serving, alongside strong multi-core processing and database operations. While it delivers top-tier prompt processing for large language models via CPU-based matrix extensions, text generation speeds remain average due to the absence of dedicated GPUs. This server is configured for compute-intensive applications, software development pipelines, and high-throughput database workloads requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier performance in compilation and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, integrated NVMe storage, and advanced matrix extensions for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in software compilation and static web serving
Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) supporting CPU-based LLM prompt processing
Strong multi-core processing and database operation capabilities
